blog_posts.title: The Web
blog_posts.context: A living collection of Mike's research, philosophical analysis, and thinking — all connected like a web. Covers topics from the Tree of Human Flourishing to computer ethics to relationship philosophy.
blog_posts.summary: The Web is Mike's public thinking space on mikeveson.com. It's a stream of research documents, philosophical analysis, personal reactions, and ideas. Posts cover ethics and technology, the Tree of Human Flourishing, relationship philosophy, and whatever Mike is currently processing. Each post can have its own visual identity through custom theming. Think of it as a raw, informal window into how Mike actually thinks — sophisticated in substance, casual in presentation.

blog_posts.title: What Are You Going to Do With Your Life?
blog_posts.context: Written for a Rice University entrepreneurship class, this post reflects on the intersection of family legacy and a personal drive for innovation in technology.
blog_posts.summary: This post explores Mike's entrepreneurial journey, deeply influenced by a family history in shipping software. It covers how observing his parents build Veson Nautical from the ground up demystified the process of starting a company. The narrative connects his early exposure to tech to his hands-on experience developing the first Veson Nautical Mobile App, illustrating a full-circle moment that solidified his passion for building impactful software.
